hero.title: Empowering Communities. Transforming Lives.
hero.subtitle: Youth for World Nepal (YWN) works directly with communities to build sustainable ways out of poverty, improve health, and encourage local leadership across rural Nepal.

trust.text: Established 2004 | CDO Regd No: 629 | KMC Ward 31 Regd: 33 | SWC Affiliation: 18085

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: 20 Years of Grassroots Impact
about.history: Founded in 2004 (2061 BS), Youth for World Nepal (YWN) is a non-profit dedicated to improving the lives of Nepal's most disadvantaged communities. We believe that development is a human right, not just charity.
about.governance: Transparency starts at the top. We are governed by a democratic General Assembly, ensuring that our direction is always dictated by the communities we serve. Day-to-day operations are strictly managed by a highly skilled Central Office in Kathmandu.
about.boardSubtitle: Guided by elite public health doctors, epidemiologists, and social scientists.
about.teamSubtitle: The operational experts executing massive rural projects.

sunlal.name: Dr. Sunlal Thapa
sunlal.desc: Leading field operations with over 33 years of experience at the Ministry of Health.

basudev.name: Mr. Basudev Khatiwada
basudev.desc: Ensuring strict compliance, logistics, and on-time project delivery across all 32+ districts.

programs.title: Our Programs & Impact
programs.subtitle: We focus on four main areas to fix the root causes of poverty.

edu.title: Education
edu.desc: Teaching people their rights and fighting illiteracy at the grassroots level.

liv.title: Livelihood
liv.desc: Promoting climate-smart farming and microfinance to create sustainable income.

health.title: Health & Nutrition
health.desc: Focusing on disease prevention, maternal health, and aggressively training local health workers.

gov.title: Governance
gov.desc: Helping citizens hold their local governments accountable for better resource distribution.

programs.projectsSubtitle: Over the last two decades, we have handled large budgets and complex projects seamlessly.

smart.title: Smart Dampati Campaign
smart.desc: Reached 81,000+ couples for family planning in direct partnership with USAID and NHEICC.

malaria.title: Malariometric Survey
malaria.desc: Successfully surveyed 5,868 samples across 32 hard-to-reach districts for the Global Fund.

lymphatic.title: Lymphatic Filariasis Survey
lymphatic.desc: Conducted deep-field research, surveying 15,000 households across 10 districts for RTI/USAID.

nutrition.title: Nutrition Programs
nutrition.desc: Handled massive maternal nutrition training across 10 districts for the Micronutrient Initiative.
